metadata.scope: Research-only ownership and migration graph for splitting portable contracts, the classic runtime, and a Bevy ECS-native runtime. Production code, manifests, features, and cassettes are unchanged.

nodes.name: rig
nodes.rationale: The facade is the only allowed convergence point for core, runtimes, and integrations.

nodes.name: rig-core
nodes.rationale: Current core mixes portable contracts, classic runtime, providers, and integrations; target core owns contracts and canonical values.

nodes.name: rig-agent
nodes.rationale: Owns the complete current runtime and hooks without semantic change during extraction.

nodes.name: rig-bevy
nodes.rationale: Isolates Bevy dependencies, ECS topology, schedules, systems, effects, policies, persistence, and handles.

nodes.name: rig-derive
nodes.rationale: Embed remains core-oriented; tool expansion must distinguish portable authoring from classic contextual execution.

nodes.name: rig-bedrock
nodes.rationale: Should depend on core contracts; runtime examples may use facade/runtime dev dependencies.

nodes.name: rig-candle
nodes.rationale: Local model implementation consumes core model contracts and should not pull either runtime.

nodes.name: rig-fastembed
nodes.rationale: Embedding implementation consumes core embedding contracts.

nodes.name: rig-gemini-grpc
nodes.rationale: Provider implementation consumes core requests and canonical responses.

nodes.name: rig-helixdb
nodes.rationale: Consumes portable vector-store contracts.

nodes.name: rig-lancedb
nodes.rationale: Consumes portable vector-store contracts.

nodes.name: rig-memory
nodes.rationale: Consumes core memory/message contracts; runtimes orchestrate load and commit timing.

nodes.name: rig-milvus
nodes.rationale: Consumes portable vector-store contracts.

nodes.name: rig-mongodb
nodes.rationale: Consumes portable vector-store contracts.

nodes.name: rig-neo4j
nodes.rationale: Consumes portable vector-store contracts.

nodes.name: rig-postgres
nodes.rationale: Consumes portable vector-store contracts.

nodes.name: rig-qdrant
nodes.rationale: Consumes portable vector-store contracts.

nodes.name: rig-s3vectors
nodes.rationale: Consumes portable vector-store contracts.

nodes.name: rig-scylladb
nodes.rationale: Consumes portable vector-store contracts.

nodes.name: rig-sqlite
nodes.rationale: Consumes portable vector-store contracts.

nodes.name: rig-surrealdb
nodes.rationale: Consumes portable vector-store contracts.

nodes.name: rig-vectorize
nodes.rationale: Consumes portable vector-store contracts.

nodes.name: rig-vertexai
nodes.rationale: Provider implementation consumes core requests and canonical responses.

nodes.name: rig_core::agent
nodes.symbol: Agent, AgentBuilder, AgentRunner, AgentRun, hooks, prompt requests
nodes.rationale: Complete current agent runtime and extension lifecycle.

nodes.name: rig_core::audio_generation
nodes.rationale: Independent provider capability.

nodes.name: rig_core::client
nodes.symbol: ProviderClient, CompletionClient, generic Client, Provider, Capabilities
nodes.proposed_owner: split between rig-core and rig-agent client extension
nodes.rationale: Model/client capability stays core; agent and extractor constructors move.

nodes.name: rig_core::completion
nodes.symbol: Message, CompletionModel, request/response/usage, Prompt, Chat, TypedPrompt
nodes.proposed_owner: split between rig-core contracts and rig-agent facade traits/errors
nodes.portability_classification: mixed contract, canonical value, and classic behavior
nodes.rationale: Low-level provider contracts are shared; prompt facade promises runtime orchestration.

nodes.name: rig_core::embeddings
nodes.symbol: EmbeddingModel, EmbeddingsBuilder, Embed, embedding tool helpers
nodes.proposed_owner: rig-core with runtime tool conveniences split out
nodes.portability_classification: mixed portable contract and runtime convenience
nodes.rationale: Embedding is portable; executable retrieval integration is runtime-specific.

nodes.name: rig_core::extractor
nodes.rationale: Implementation owns an Agent, output-tool recovery, retries, and hooks.

nodes.name: rig_core::http_client
nodes.symbol: HttpClientExt, Request, Response, SSE, retry, multipart
nodes.rationale: Providers need transport without either runtime.

nodes.name: rig_core::id
nodes.rationale: IDs participate in provider canonical round trips.

nodes.name: rig_core::image_generation
nodes.rationale: Independent provider capability.

nodes.name: rig_core::integrations
nodes.rationale: Current integrations import and store the classic Agent.

nodes.name: rig_core::loaders
nodes.rationale: Runtime-neutral and outside the split scope.

nodes.name: rig_core::markers
nodes.rationale: Used by low-level provider request builders.

nodes.name: rig_core::memory
nodes.proposed_owner: rig-core contracts/backend; runtime orchestration split out
nodes.portability_classification: mixed backend contract and runtime behavior
nodes.rationale: Storage operations are portable; load/append timing belongs to runtimes.

nodes.name: rig_core::model
nodes.rationale: Provider capability independent of runtimes.

nodes.name: rig_core::one_or_many
nodes.rationale: Non-empty collection used by canonical content.

nodes.name: rig_core::prelude
nodes.symbol: client, agent, prompt, streaming, embedding, tool, vector re-exports
nodes.proposed_owner: split core, rig-agent, rig-bevy, and root facade preludes
nodes.rationale: Current prelude mixes contracts and classic runtime and would collide with Bevy ergonomics.

nodes.name: rig_core::providers
nodes.proposed_owner: provider integration crates after the runtime split
nodes.rationale: Not provider-neutral, but movement is an orthogonal follow-up to preserve reviewability.

nodes.name: rig_core::rerank
nodes.rationale: Independent provider capability.

nodes.name: rig_core::streaming
nodes.proposed_owner: raw provider primitives in rig-core; runtime facades in rig-agent
nodes.portability_classification: mixed canonical/provider values and classic facade
nodes.rationale: Raw streams are shared; high-level traits return classic runtime request types.

nodes.name: rig_core::test_utils
nodes.visibility: public under test or test-utils feature
nodes.proposed_owner: split core mocks, rig-agent tests, and test-only runtime conformance
nodes.rationale: Current helpers mix provider/model and agent-runtime scenarios.

nodes.name: rig_core::tool
nodes.symbol: Tool, ToolEmbedding, ToolOutput, ToolResult, ToolContext, ToolSet, DynamicTool, server
nodes.proposed_owner: portable authoring/canonical values in core; classic execution in rig-agent; ECS execution in rig-bevy
nodes.portability_classification: mixed contract, value, and runtime infrastructure
nodes.rationale: Highest-risk internal split; runtime contexts and registries must not constrain both runtimes.

nodes.name: rig_core::transcription
nodes.rationale: Independent provider capability.

nodes.name: rig_core::vector_store
nodes.symbol: VectorStoreIndex, VectorStoreIndexDyn, filters, requests, in-memory store, tool integration
nodes.proposed_owner: portable store contracts in core; automatic runtime tool adapters split out
nodes.portability_classification: mixed backend contract and runtime convenience
nodes.rationale: Search is portable; advertising/executing it as a tool belongs to runtimes.

nodes.name: rig_core::wasm_compat
nodes.rationale: Prevents native runtime requirements from leaking into core/provider APIs.

nodes.name: rig_core::telemetry
nodes.proposed_owner: provider semantic helpers in core; runtime spans split to runtime crates
nodes.portability_classification: mixed provider helper and classic runtime telemetry
nodes.rationale: Current builder recognizes the classic agent span target.

nodes.name: CompletionClient
nodes.proposed_owner: completion_model in rig-core; agent/extractor in rig-agent::AgentClientExt
nodes.portability_classification: mixed client contract and facade convenience
nodes.rationale: Direct current boundary crossing from core client to classic builders.

nodes.name: Completion contracts
nodes.rationale: Stable provider/runtime boundary with typed raw response.

nodes.name: Canonical messages and content
nodes.symbol: Message, UserContent, AssistantContent, ToolCall, ToolResult, media/document content
nodes.rationale: Providers and both runtimes exchange these values.

nodes.name: Classic invocation facade
nodes.rationale: Promises tool execution, transcript mutation, memory, budgets, cancellation, and structured recovery.

nodes.name: Raw streaming contracts
nodes.rationale: Provider implementations and direct consumers need raw/typed final streaming.

nodes.name: Classic Agent runtime
nodes.symbol: Agent, AgentBuilder, AgentRunner, AgentRun, AgentRunStep, prompt request/response types
nodes.rationale: Owns classic progression and should move intact before refactoring.

nodes.name: Classic hook system
nodes.symbol: AgentHook, HookStack, HookContext, lifecycle events/actions, RequestPatch, Scratchpad
nodes.rationale: Event-specific steering and composition depend on classic lifecycle.

nodes.name: Model-turn response retry
nodes.visibility: public hook action with runtime implementation
nodes.proposed_owner: rig-agent with equivalent rig-bevy conformance behavior
nodes.portability_classification: classic behavior and shared observable specification
nodes.rationale: Merged PR #2182 behavior is current oracle and absent from the older ECS experiment base.

nodes.name: Portable tool authoring and values
nodes.symbol: Tool, ToolEmbedding, ToolDefinition, ToolOutput, IntoToolOutput, ToolExecutionError, ToolErrorKind
nodes.rationale: Both runtimes need authoring metadata, typed outputs, and normalized failures.

nodes.name: Classic tool execution infrastructure
nodes.symbol: ToolContext, ToolSet, ToolSetBuilder, ToolServer, ToolServerHandle, ErasedTool, registry snapshots and dispatch
nodes.rationale: Registry, context, live server, snapshots, parsing, and execution sequencing are runtime-specific.

nodes.name: Vector-store contracts
nodes.rationale: Companion stores and both runtimes consume these contracts.

nodes.name: Conversation memory contracts
nodes.rationale: Backends store canonical messages independently of runtime progression.

nodes.name: Classic extractor
nodes.rationale: Agent-based retry and output-tool orchestration is not a low-level completion contract.

nodes.name: Provider telemetry helpers
nodes.symbol: CompletionOperation, CompletionSpanBuilder provider fields, ProviderResponseExt, SpanCombinator
nodes.rationale: Provider GenAI metadata is shared; runtime span ownership must split.

nodes.name: Provider response preservation
nodes.rationale: Both runtimes forward preserved provider errors through their own error types.

nodes.name: Structured-output mode and recovery boundary
nodes.current_owner: rig-core agent runtime plus a method on the core CompletionModel trait
nodes.proposed_owner: OutputMode/recovery in rig-agent; a narrowly named provider capability in rig-core
nodes.portability_classification: mixed provider capability and classic runtime behavior
nodes.rationale: Provider composition support is portable evidence, while selecting modes, synthetic tools, rollback, and recovery are runtime progression.

nodes.name: Bevy topology and schedules
nodes.symbol: components, relationships, stable IDs, schedules, systems, progress/quiescence
nodes.rationale: Must be authoritative ECS state, not a mirror of AgentRun.

nodes.name: Bevy owned effect boundary
nodes.symbol: owned model/tool/store effects, completion ingress, generations, cancellation, stale-result handling
nodes.rationale: No world borrow enters futures; only systems mutate authoritative state.

nodes.name: Bevy policy and capability system
nodes.symbol: policy components/systems, capability and grant entities, ordered decisions
nodes.rationale: ECS-native extension mechanism must not wrap HookStack.

nodes.name: Bevy persistence and debugging
nodes.symbol: explicit domain snapshots, rebinding, retirement, explanation/debug views
nodes.rationale: Persist stable canonical records, never raw World or runtime-only implementations.

nodes.name: Current model conformance helpers
nodes.proposed_owner: split core provider fixtures and shared runtime scenario package
nodes.rationale: Existing tests supply observable behavior but currently mix test layers.

nodes.name: Classic runtime unit/parity tests
nodes.symbol: blocking/streaming, hooks, retries, concurrency, telemetry tests
nodes.rationale: Behavior oracle for semantic-preserving extraction.

nodes.name: Provider cassette tests and fixtures
nodes.rationale: Run once per provider mapping; do not rerecord for runtime movement.

nodes.name: Shared runtime conformance scenarios
nodes.symbol: scripted effects, canonical transcripts/events, private harness adapter
nodes.rationale: Constrains observable parity without a production shared state machine.

nodes.name: Workspace agent/examples import surface
nodes.symbol: 70 example files with direct rig/rig_core runtime-bearing references
nodes.proposed_owner: explicit classic or Bevy example surfaces
nodes.rationale: Examples define practical migration paths and must select a runtime explicitly.

nodes.name: External tests referencing classic runtime surfaces
nodes.symbol: 256 root and rig-derive test files with direct rig/rig_core runtime-bearing references
nodes.current_owner: provider, integration, core, fixture, and derive tests
nodes.proposed_owner: split provider mapping, rig-agent, shared conformance, integration, and derive test layers
nodes.portability_classification: mixed provider and runtime testing infrastructure
nodes.rationale: Import ownership must be separated without rerecording provider cassettes or moving wire assertions into a runtime crate.

nodes.name: WASM-compatible portable bounds
nodes.symbol: target-selective Send, Sync, boxed future, stream, timeout
nodes.proposed_owner: rig-core reused by rig-agent; rig-bevy may add stricter adapter bounds
nodes.rationale: ECS/native executor requirements must not weaken portable provider/tool/storage contracts.

nodes.name: ECS PR #6 experiment
nodes.rationale: Provides ECS concepts and boundary failure evidence but is not a specification.

edges.migration_implication: Facade remains an allowed consumer of core.

evidence.anchor: features table; complete map in runtime-import-inventory.md

evidence.symbol: Agent implementations of Prompt, Chat, and TypedPrompt

edges.migration_implication: Move the facade traits, their Agent implementations, and runtime request types together to rig-agent.
Keep raw provider streaming values core and move the multi-turn facade to rig-agent.

evidence.symbol: AgentImpl imports Agent, MultiTurnStreamItem, and StreamingPrompt

edges.migration_implication: Move CLI and Discord agent helpers to the classic runtime or facade integration layer.

evidence.symbol: Agent, prompting, streaming, and ToolSet re-exports

edges.migration_implication: Create a portable core prelude and explicit runtime preludes; keep the root facade prelude classic-compatible.
Keep portable embedding values core and move dynamic tool erasure/registry coupling to a runtime adapter.
Move method to rig-agent extension trait.
Move method to rig-agent extension trait.
Remove the provider inherent method and provide blanket rig-agent::model::AgentModelExt instead; provider code must remain runtime-free.

edges.migration_implication: Becomes rig-agent -> rig-core dependency.
Keep the provider capability boolean core; move mode selection and recovery state to rig-agent.
Move both to rig-agent.
Preserve classic implementation; specify equivalent Bevy behavior through conformance.

evidence.symbol: ErasedTool/ToolSet dispatch typed Tool and ToolOutput

edges.migration_implication: Invert as rig-agent -> rig-core.

edges.migration_implication: Implement in ECS vertical slices.
No ECS borrow enters futures; generation checked on ingress.
ECS policy is independent of classic hooks.
Persist explicit stable records and rebind implementations.
Move classic history and turn state with the classic runtime instead of leaving runtime ownership in core.
Serialize explicit versioned domain records, never Bevy World internals or implementation handles.
Classic runtime implements private dev harness.
Bevy runtime implements private dev harness without shared production engine.
Compatibility cannot be implemented in core.
Core remains runtime-neutral.
No Bevy types, derives, entities, or schedules in core public APIs.
No ECS adapter around HookStack or AgentRun.
Bevy owns independent progression and policy.
